response: We propose a new method for [task/domain], aiming to improve upon existing baselines. Evaluated on the standard test set across five random seeds, our method achieves an average accuracy of 73.4 ± 1.1%, compared to a strong baseline at 72.2 ± 0.9%, representing a +1.2 percentage point improvement in absolute accuracy. A two-sample t-test indicates that this difference is not statistically significant (t = 1.04, p = 0.31), with a 95% confidence interval on the gain of [–1.1, +3.5]. These results suggest that while our method attains a numerically higher mean accuracy, the improvement is within the range of random variation under current experimental conditions. Our findings highlight the importance of careful statistical analysis and adequate replication when assessing incremental advances in [task/domain] performance.
